programmes.show_description: Alf complains to the DHSS that he should have been paid more winter's heating allowance. He has a loud revealing dream in front of his friends. S3 E6
With Cliff Richard and Dave Edmunds and Rockville. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
A slimy gossip columnist is killed and it's up to Simon to find the true murderer from the list of subjects. S1 E7: The Arrow of God

After WWII, Britain rushed the Comet jetliner into service. Mysterious crashes led to tests that reshaped aircraft design forever. Narrated by Robert Powell.
The Allied fronts in both western and eastern Europe had become faster-moving by 1944 - a new form of aircraft was needed to deliver swift powerful attacks against German lines. Ep5: Fighter Bombers
We take a look at one of WWII's most significant theatres of warfare, in which Germany's U-boat fleet relentlessly attacked Britain's supply lines. S1 E6: Battle of the Atlantic
Despite the general inexperience of its young pilots, the Royal Air Force Fighter Command ultimately prevailed in its battle against the German Luftwaffe. Ep6: RAF Fighter Command
The F-15 Eagle immediately turned the tide of the first Gulf War to prove its greatness, and was involved in one of the most bizarre combat incidents during that conflict. S2 E6
Devastation touches the civilians of Germany; with the US as a newly-engaged ally, Western forces bring the war to the Axis front but at extreme costs. Ep5: Bombing The Reich
The campaign in Tunisia in World War Two saw the battles that finally ended the brutal war in the desert. We look at the weapons used and tactics and tell the story of the battle itself.
Examining the history of war tactics. We explore the history and principles of guerrilla warfare. S1 E12
The story of the boffins and experimenters who developed the first workable radar system in time to help defeat the Nazi attacks in the Battle of Britain. Ep1: The Men Who Invented Radar

programmes.show_name: Margaret Thatcher: The Lady's Not for Turning
programmes.show_description: We examine the life and career of Margaret Thatcher, Britain's first female Prime Minister and one of the dominant political figures of 20th-century Britain.
...Where Were You? There are some news events that change the world. In today's episode we take you back to July 25th 2000 and the fatal crash of the Air France Concorde flight 4590.
Two of the greatest failed invasions in the history of naval warfare - how the Spanish Armada was defeated and why the Allied fleet was crushed in WWI - are explored. Ep6: Failed Invasions
Ordered to attack the St Nazaire U-boat base, British commandos undertook 'the greatest raid of all' by turning an old destroyer into a live bomb to ram the gates. Ep4: Storm at St. Nazaire
Jack is in demand to speak up for Geordie Watson, the local Labour candidate in a forthcoming election. S2 E11: The Way It Was in Murmansk
When Arthur, Dave and Ray take a trip across the English Channel to take advantage of duty-free prices, it's Arthur who ultimately gets taken advantage of. S10 E2: Another Case of Van Blank
As Elizabeth becomes more ambivalent about Valentine, George courts Sir Francis Basset for the vacant magistracy position. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E2
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. When CID hear of a dead miner, all roads lead to the notorious cop killer Leslie Johns. Starring John Simm. S2 E8
Christmas is a time to be with the family, but not for Alf Garnett. He is destined to spend Christmas in hospital, having his hip replaced. In Sickness and In Health Christmas Special 1987
With Dusty Springfield, Elvis Costello and The Police. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon settles an account with a corrupt American attorney, using his own methods of justice to do so. S1 E8: The Element of Doubt

By 1915 trench warfare had stalled, Naval and amphibious plans had failed disastrously, revealing why the Dardanelles campaign collapsed. Narrated by Robert Powell.
At the outbreak of WWII, the only heavy four-engined bombers in the war were either Russian or American - soon the Short Stirling, the Avro Lancaster, and the Liberator were added. Ep6: Heavy Bombers
After Hitler invaded Poland, the Western Allies and Nazi Germany were technically at war - for 7 months, neither side engaged in major military land operations. S1 E7: 'Sitzkrieg' and the Phoney War
One of the world's elite WWII fighting forces, the Commandos were created by Churchill in the summer of 1940 as a means of striking at the coasts of Nazi-occupied Europe. Ep7: The Paras and Commandos
The Hurricane will be remembered for the vital role it played in WWII, and how it destroyed more enemy aircraft during the Battle of Britain than all other air and ground defences combined. S3 E1
Japan begins to conquer southeast Asia as the Allies attempt to block the empire's aggressive expansion, but ruthless tactics by Japan only help to further their war effort. Ep6: Road to Burma

We examine the course of savage fighting in the Balkans in the latter half of the conflict in World War 2 where after the uprising by the Romanians, the Soviet army moved swiftly to capture Bucharest.
Examining the history of war tactics. Urban warfare is the most vicious form of fighting and takes a soldier back to the most primitive type of warfare: hand-to-hand. S1 E13
The story of RJ Mitchell and his team who developed the Spitfire just in time to defeat the Nazi attacks during the Battle of Britain. Ep2: The Man Who Designed The Spitfire
A look into the lives of the rich and famous - this time we look at Oprah Winfrey, America's first lady of celebrity.
...You? There are some news events that change the world - we take you back to the 26th of December, 2004 and the Indonesian tsunami.
The evolution of the Vietnam conflict from a regional military engagement to an ever-expanding war, here exploring how it was logistically possible and a look at the Huey helicopter and its role.
Investigations reveal the greatest scuttle and act of defiance in wartime history as testimonies and CGI illuminate the events and subsequent salvage. Ep7: The Lost Fleet of WWI
The union head office wants Jack to force Colefax - a local employer - to allow it into his factory, but Jack has split loyalties; also, Bella is taken ill. S2 E12: In the Front Line You Get Shot At
Arthur's chum Sidney comes to him for protection from a murderer, but Arthur's idea of a safe house is a caravan belonging to Dave's sister. S10 E3: All Things Brighton Beautiful
Ross attempts to learn of Dwight's fate in Revolutionary France. In Cornwall, George and Elizabeth lock horns with Sam and Drake. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E3
Drama series following the exploits of old-school detective Gene Hunt from the drama series Life on Mars. DI Alex Drake is shot and lands in 1981 before coming face-to-face with Hunt. S1 E1
